text:
Once upon a time, in a sunflower field so wide you could swim across it, there lived a golden sunflower named Sunbeam. Sunbeam radiated sunshine, not just a little bit.

One bright morning, while humming a tune about happiness, Sunbeam noticed something peculiar. Several shadow butterflies flitted around them, unusually quiet and cautious. Sunbeam didn’t think much of it; maybe they loved whispers.

Later, Sunbeam spotted the butterflies under a large oak leaf, discussing something important in their own language that only bees could understand. Intrigued, Sunbeam approached.

As she got closer, the bees buzzed away mysteriously. Undeterred, Sunbeam befriended them, learning their ways. They showed her how to fold leaves into wings. Sunbeam’s newfound ability became legendary in the field.

One day, a giant shadow moth descended upon the field. It wasn’t just big; it was fearsome. Sunbeam knew she had to help. With her
